schemes.name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
schemes.description: Provides ₹6,000 per year to small and marginal farmers.
schemes.eligibility: Must be a landholding farmer family.
schemes.howToApply: Register on pmkisan.gov.in or visit nearest CSC.

schemes.name: MGNREGA
schemes.description: Guarantees 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to a rural household.
schemes.eligibility: Any adult member of a rural household willing to do unskilled manual work.
schemes.howToApply: Apply at Gram Panchayat with a photograph.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana
schemes.description: Maternity benefit of ₹5,000 for pregnant women and lactating mothers.
schemes.eligibility: First live birth, excluding regular govt employees.
schemes.howToApply: Apply at Anganwadi Centre or ASHA worker.

schemes.name: e-Shram
schemes.description: National database of unorganized workers, provides ₹2 lakh accidental insurance.
schemes.eligibility: Age 16-59, not a member of EPFO/ESIC.
schemes.howToApply: Register online at eshram.gov.in or CSC.

Women.title: Protection from Domestic Violence
Women.desc: The Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005 protects women from physical, emotional, verbal, sexual, and economic abuse at home.
Women.fullDetails:
What is covered?
Any act of physical, emotional, sexual, verbal, or economic abuse. This also includes dowry-related harassment.

Who can complain?
Any woman who is or has been in a domestic relationship with the abuser (wife, sister, mother, live-in partner).

Reliefs available:
  • Protection orders to prevent further violence.
  • Residence orders (right to live in the shared household).
  • Monetary relief for expenses and losses.
  • Custody of children.

How to complain: You can reach out to the local Protection Officer or Police, or call the Women's Helpline (1091).

Women.title: Equal Pay for Equal Work
Women.desc: Under the Equal Remuneration Act, women must be paid the same as men for the same work.
Women.fullDetails:
The Law:
The Equal Remuneration Act, 1976 mandates the payment of equal remuneration to men and women workers for the same work or work of a similar nature.

Key Features:
  • Employers cannot discriminate during recruitment or promotions on the basis of gender.
  • Wages cannot be reduced for any worker to equalize pay.
  • Violations can lead to strict penalties for the employer.

Women.title: Maternity Leave
Women.desc: The Maternity Benefit Act allows up to 26 weeks of paid maternity leave.
Women.fullDetails:
Eligibility:
A woman must have worked in the establishment for at least 80 days in the past 12 months.

Benefits:
  • Up to 26 weeks of paid leave for the first two children.
  • 12 weeks for subsequent children.
  • Special provisions for adopting mothers or commissioning mothers (12 weeks).
  • A medical bonus and nursing breaks during working hours upon returning.

Employers are prohibited from dismissing a woman solely because she is on maternity leave.

Farmer.title: MSP Guarantee (Administrative)
Farmer.desc: Farmers have the right to sell crops at Minimum Support Price (MSP) in designated APMC mandis.

Farmer.title: Right to Claim Crop Insurance
Farmer.desc: Under PM Fasal Bima Yojana, farmers can claim insurance for crop loss due to natural calamities.

Labor.title: Minimum Wages Act
Labor.desc: Every worker is entitled to receive the minimum wage fixed by the state government.

Labor.title: Regulated Working Hours
Labor.desc: Standard 8 hours a day. Overtime must be paid at double the ordinary rate of wages.

Consumer.title: Right to Information
Consumer.desc: Consumers have the right to be informed about the quality, quantity, price, and purity of goods.

Consumer.title: Right to Consumer Courts
Consumer.desc: You can file a complaint in consumer forums if you receive defective goods or deficient services.
